sources: docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6e S2 eligibleAt(anchor,H,K):=H>=h+K, S5 (K,W,C) per-network params (candidate; da-windows #49)
docs/core/DECISIONS.md entry 49 da-windows (PROVISIONAL pending DK) S2/S5
docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md Z4 sources (DA §6a candidate) + Blocked on decision: da-windows

inputs.attackFlagText: K is unpinned (da-windows); a small K re-exposes the §3 boundary divergence the lag exists to absorb — parameter choice is consensus-critical, not tuning.
inputs.predicateSignature: confirmedRootMembership(anchor, depth, K[#49 provisional=6], witnessedChain{reorgHistory}, priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseDepthKminus1Rejected: an anchor at depth K-1 (provisional K=6) contributing to the confirmed root -> REJECT (not yet eligible; eligibleAt H>=h+K, #49 S2)
fixture.caseDepthKAccepted: the same anchor at depth K -> accept (now eligible / in the confirmed root) — the companion positive
fixture.caseSubKReorgInvariant: a reorg replacing only blocks shallower than K leaves the confirmed-root sequence unchanged (sub-K churn does not move eligibility)
fixture.K: #49 S5 per-network parameter passed as kernel input (no baked constant); provisional value 6

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): the verdict (an anchor at depth K-1 contributes nothing to the confirmed root; at depth K it does; the confirmed-root sequence is invariant under sub-K reorgs) is derivable from DA §6a/§6e S2 (eligibleAt H>=h+K), but K is the #49 per-network parameter passed as input (S5) — PROVISIONAL pending DK. K-as-input (not a baked constant) is itself the #49 ruling, and K-choice is consensus-critical (a small K re-exposes the §3 boundary divergence). Depends on #49 ⇒ provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; re-parameterizes on DK's #49 K ruling.

expected.invariant: an anchor at depth K-1 contributes nothing to the confirmed root (reject; eligibleAt H>=h+K, #49 S2), at depth K it does, and a reorg replacing only sub-K-deep blocks leaves the confirmed-root sequence unchanged; K is a per-network input (#49 S5), not a baked constant; identical on deterministic replay
expected.rationale: Z4's core verdict — an anchor at depth K-1 contributes nothing to the confirmed root; at depth K it does; confirmed-root sequence invariant under sub-K reorgs — is derivable from DA §6a + §6e (S2 eligibleAt H >= h+K), but that algebra is provisional on da-windows (#49, PROVISIONAL pending DK). K being a per-network parameter passed as input (S5), not a baked constant, is itself the #49 ruling. So the verdict is derivable but rides on the provisional decision; the consensus-criticality of K-choice is exactly the #49 parameters-as-inputs point.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK retunes or bakes #49's K (e.g. ratifies a fixed launch constant or a different eligibility invariant), the K value and the depth-K membership boundary re-pin to the ratified form; the eligibleAt(H>=h+K) + sub-K-reorg-invariant structure is stable, so the vector re-parameterizes to the new K-as-input form.

sources: docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6e S1 one clock — mined height in current best chain, reorgs re-derive, no receipt-time (candidate; da-windows #49)
docs/core/DECISIONS.md entry 47 marker-fold (RATIFIED) — windows key off anchor's mined height; entry 49 da-windows (PROVISIONAL)

inputs.attackFlagText: Whether the qualifying-bond test reads the bond's own mined height on the current chain (re-derived) or its first-seen height is unstated — same reorg-to-extend family as Z5.
inputs.predicateSignature: qualifyingBondTest(bond, minedHeightCurrentChain[re-derived per #49 S1], witnessedChain{reorgHistory}, priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseFirstSeenHeightRejected: a qualifying-bond test that reads the bond's FIRST-SEEN height (rather than the re-derived current-chain mined height) -> REJECT as non-conformant (#49 S1 one-clock: all heights from the current best chain, reorgs re-derive, no receipt-time input)
fixture.caseReDerivedAccepts: the outcome re-derived purely from the current-canonical-chain bond set (current-chain mined heights) -> accept — the companion positive
fixture.scopeExclusion: the earliest-vs-current re-mine PRIORITY is a separate, still-spec-blocked question (the other Z5/Z9 flags) and is NOT asserted here; this vector tests only the current-chain-vs-first-seen height SOURCE
fixture.priorState: a bond whose containing block was reorged and re-mined at a different height

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): whether the qualifying-bond test reads the bond's re-derived current-chain mined height vs its first-seen height is answered by the #49 S1 one-clock rule (all deadlines from the anchor's mined height in the current best chain; reorgs re-derive; no receipt-time) combined with ratified marker-fold (#47, mined-height keying). The bond reads re-derived current-chain mined height, not first-seen — derivable but the algebra is provisional on #49. The earliest-vs-current re-mine priority remains separately spec-blocked (other Z5/Z9 flags); NOT asserted here. Depends on #49 ⇒ provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; re-anchors on DK's #49 S1 one-clock ruling.

expected.invariant: the qualifying-bond test reads the bond's re-derived mined height on the current best chain (#49 S1 one-clock; reorgs re-derive; no receipt-time), so a test reading the bond's first-seen height is non-conformant and rejected; the outcome re-derives purely from the current-canonical-chain bond set; identical on deterministic replay. (The earliest-vs-current re-mine priority is a separate spec-blocked question, not decided here.)
expected.rationale: Whether the qualifying-bond test reads the bond's own mined height on the CURRENT chain (re-derived) vs first-seen height is answered by the da-windows one-clock rule (§6e S1: all deadlines from the anchor's mined height in the current best chain; reorgs re-derive h; no receipt-time input) combined with marker-fold (#47, ratified) keying off mined height. The bond's qualification reads re-derived current-chain mined height, not first-seen. This is derivable but the algebra is provisional on da-windows (#49). The 'same reorg-to-extend family as Z5' note is the spec-blocked re-mine-priority piece (separate flag), but the basic current-chain-vs-first-seen choice flows from #49 S1.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK flips #49's one-clock / parameters-as-inputs framing (e.g. admits a receipt-time or first-seen height source), the height-source semantics re-anchor to the ratified form and the first-seen-vs-re-derived verdict flips accordingly; the re-derive-from-current-canonical-chain shape is stable, so the vector stays testable under the new height-source rule.

sources: docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6e S6 'K >= W+C, K>=1, W>=1, C>=1' and the include-then-retract foreclosure (candidate; da-windows #49)
docs/core/DECISIONS.md entry 49 da-windows (PROVISIONAL pending DK) S5/S6/S7 — params as inputs, S6-violation reject + two-parameterization vectors required
docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md Z13 Step-2/Step-3 restatement — restated to strong form per §6e; Blocked on decision: da-windows

inputs.attackFlagText: If only W <= K is ratified (without K >= W + C), the challenge window C can extend past K-depth: a batch becomes confirmed-root-eligible while its fail-closed challenge is still open — the verdict and the confirmation boundary cross, exactly the ordering bug the prototype's stronger invariant prevents.
inputs.predicateSignature: daKernelConstruct(K, W, C) -> {verdict}; daVerdict parameterized by (K,W,C) inputs (no baked constant)

fixture.caseKeqWplusCminus1Rejected: construction with K = W+C-1 -> REJECT at parameter validation (#49 S6 strong form K >= W + C); the lower-bound violations W=0 / C=0 / K=0 are likewise rejected (S6: K>=1, W>=1, C>=1)
fixture.caseDualParameterizationConsistent: two distinct valid (K,W,C) parameterizations both satisfying K>=W+C are accepted with boundary-consistent includable / holdsPriority / eligibleAt verdicts -> no baked constant passes both
fixture.params: #49 S5/S6/S7: (K,W,C) inputs, strong form K>=W+C load-bearing, S6-violation reject + two-parameterization vectors required

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): Z13 was RESTATED in step-3 to the strong form per §6e S6 (K >= W+C; the weak W<=K is implied-by and superseded-by the strong form). The S6-reject vectors (K=W+C-1 rejected, lower-bound violations rejected) and the dual-parameterization vectors (#49 S7) are derivable from §6e, but §6e is da-windows (#49), PROVISIONAL pending DK. NOT retired-by-restatement: the restatement made it derivable but provisional. This is the Z-area (reorg/replay-determinism) instance of the same S6/S5/S7 family as D9 (S6) and D12 (S5/S7). Depends on #49 ⇒ provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; inverts on DK's #49 S6 strong-form ruling.

expected.invariant: the DA kernel enforces #49 S6 at construction: K = W+C-1 (weak-form violation) is rejected, as are the lower-bound violations W=0 / C=0 / K=0; two distinct valid K>=W+C parameterizations both yield boundary-consistent verdicts so no baked constant passes both; identical on deterministic replay. Forecloses the include-then-retract ordering bug where C extends past K-depth.
expected.rationale: Z13 was RESTATED in step-3 per da-windows (#49): DA §6e S6 pins the strong K >= W + C invariant (the weak W <= K is 'implied by, and superseded by, the strong form'), and S5 makes (K,W,C) inputs (no baked constant). The flag's hazard (weak-form-only lets C extend past K-depth so a batch becomes confirmed-root-eligible while its fail-closed challenge is still open) is exactly the ordering bug the strong invariant forecloses — derivable from §6e, but §6e is da-windows (#49) which is PROVISIONAL pending DK. So the S6-reject vectors (K = W+C-1 rejected, etc.) and dual-parameterization vectors are derivable but ride on #49. This is NOT retired-by-restatement: the restatement made it derivable but provisional, and the S6-reject + dual-param vectors land in step 4 per #49 S7.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK ratifies only the weak W<=K form (without K>=W+C) or bakes fixed constants, the S6-strong-form reject vectors invert (K=W+C-1 becomes admissible, reopening include-then-retract) and the dual-parameterization requirement drops; the parameter-validation predicate shape is stable, so the vector stays testable under the weak form (asserting the opposite construction verdict).
